file.content:
- **Forcing an unauthed app state for mobile-mcp verification requires BOTH app-terminate AND keychain reset before relaunch.** `expo-secure-store` is iOS Keychain backed; `xcrun simctl keychain <udid> reset` only clears the keychain persistence layer, not the in-memory AuthProvider state of a running app. If the app is merely backgrounded, the `cachedToken` module-scope variable in `src/lib/storage.ts` + the React `user` state in `AuthProvider` both survive — and on foreground the AuthProvider does NOT re-read SecureStore (hydration runs once on mount). Correct sequence: `mobile_terminate_app(host.exp.Exponent)` → `xcrun simctl keychain <udid> reset` → `mobile_launch_app(host.exp.Exponent)` → tap the project in Expo Go's Recently Opened. The re-launch then mounts a fresh AuthProvider which hydrates from the (now empty) keychain and renders the role picker. Required for any story that verifies an auth-gated screen by logging out from outside the app (US-033 duplicate-username 409 test, and whenever US-037/US-038 UI lands before a sign-out-in-app flow exists).
- **mobile-mcp `type_keys` drops the first character or two of some inputs non-deterministically.** Observed on iPhone 17 Pro sim (iOS 26.3) with Expo Go — e.g. `u33_demo_17` → `u3_demo_17`, `dup-attempt@example.com` → `d-attempt@example.com`. If the typed value needs to match exactly (e.g. duplicate-conflict test), verify via `mobile_list_elements_on_screen` after each field and correct using `mobile_double_tap_on_screen` (triggers iOS word-selection menu) → tap "Select All" → retype. Don't rely on visual screenshot inspection to confirm input — the kerning hides a dropped char.
- **Defense-in-depth CHECK constraints mirror edge-function input regexes.** Edge functions that validate user-shaped strings (handles, usernames, slugs) via a `const RE = /^.../` before inserting are the first gate, but service_role writers (admin scripts, later migrations seeding fixtures, future edge functions reusing the same table) bypass that validation. Add a matching `CHECK (col::text ~ '<same-pattern>')` constraint on the column so the shape guarantee becomes a database invariant instead of a caller-by-caller convention. Before applying, run `select ... where col !~ '<pattern>'` and fix offenders — the ALTER will fail otherwise. Ref: `social_links_handle_format_chk` in `us_035_social_links_handle_check.sql` mirrors `HANDLE_RE` in `manage-social-link`/`auth-signup-creator`. Applies to any new user-string column (lister company slug, campaign slug, etc.).
- **RLS self-read policies must cover EVERY status the UI needs to surface, not just the happy path.** The `metric_snapshots_self_read` policy originally required `status='fresh'`, but us_011's hourly cron flips `is_latest` rows from `fresh` → `stale` in place — so the creator's "Stale" chip query returned zero rows (RLS silently filtered) even when the snapshot legitimately existed. Rule: audit every status enum on any RLS-gated table against the UI states that need to render it, and widen the policy accordingly. Keep `service_role`-only states (in-flight `refreshing`, error `failed`) off the self-read policy — those states are communicated to the client through edge-function responses, not direct table reads. Ref: `us_035_metric_snapshots_self_read_stale.sql`.
- **TabBar missing from a11y tree on some screens — use coord-clicks as the verified workaround.** `mobile_list_elements_on_screen` doesn't always report the custom `TabBar` (src/components/shared/TabBar.tsx) tab buttons inside nested route groups (observed on `/feed` during US-035 verification). The buttons are rendered and tappable, but absent from the accessibility tree snapshot. Workaround: take a screenshot to visually confirm the tab bar is present, then `mobile_click_on_screen_at_coordinates` at the known tab positions (on iPhone 17 Pro sim: ~y=820, x ≈ 44/130/217/303/352 for a 5-tab bar — inspect the screenshot for exact coords per layout). Don't block UI verification on missing a11y entries; confirm via screenshot + tap.
- **Supabase Realtime requires an explicit `supabase.realtime.setAuth(token)` before `channel().subscribe()` under the custom-JWT wrapper pattern.** `src/lib/supabase.ts` overrides `global.fetch` with `marketifyFetch` so HTTP requests carry our HS256 `MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET` token — but Realtime opens a separate websocket (`/realtime/v1`) that does NOT go through the fetch wrapper. Without `setAuth`, the websocket either auths as anon (no rows visible under RLS) or fails handshake entirely. Pattern: `await supabase.realtime.setAuth(getCachedToken()); channel = supabase.channel(...).on('postgres_changes', ...).subscribe();` then `removeChannel(channel)` in cleanup. Gate the whole block on a `cancelled` flag because setAuth is async and the effect may re-run or unmount before it resolves. Ref: `app/(creator)/profile.tsx` US-036 realtime effect.
- **Synchronous `useRef` lock for rapid-fire gesture handlers where React state deps would stale-capture.** When a `useCallback` has `[stateFlag, ...]` in its deps and is invoked by a gesture/press that can fire multiple times in the same render tick, the closure captures the PREVIOUS `stateFlag` — two pulls in a row both see `false` and both run. Pattern: pair a `useRef(false)` that the callback reads and writes synchronously (`if (lock.current) return; lock.current = true; ... finally { lock.current = false }`) with the React state that drives UI. Remove the state flag from the callback's deps since the ref is the actual guard. Same stale-capture mechanism as Pattern #82's Reanimated worklets applies to rapid-fire JS-side handlers (pull-to-refresh, double-press submits, scroll-triggered loads). Ref: `app/(creator)/profile.tsx` `refreshLock` in US-036.
- **Pan gesture coexisting with a parent ScrollView: use `activeOffsetY([15, 9999])` + `failOffsetX([-20, 20])` instead of `activateAfterLongPress`.** Long-press activation is unreliable when the user starts dragging quickly — ScrollView's native gesture wins and the Pan never fires. The threshold-based activation per RNGH canonical docs declares "only downward pulls past 15pt count as pan" without stealing all touches: upward scrolls and horizontal drifts are rejected, so the ScrollView handles normal content scroll, and a deliberate downward pull triggers the Pan. `failOffsetX` prevents a diagonal swipe from being misread as a pull. Inside `.onUpdate`, apply a piecewise-linear damping past threshold (`raw <= THRESHOLD ? raw : THRESHOLD + (raw - THRESHOLD) * 0.35`) for the rubber-band feel. Ref: `RefreshablePlatformCard` in `app/(creator)/profile.tsx`.
- **Supabase MCP `deploy_edge_function` bundles files exactly as you name them.** The entrypoint MUST be named `source/<entrypoint>.ts` (not `index.ts`) and the `entrypoint_path` field must match (`source/index.ts`). Shared modules live at bundle root as `_shared/<name>.ts` — NOT `source/_shared/<name>.ts`. The entrypoint imports them as `../_shared/<name>.ts` because the bundler resolves relative to `source/`. Mismatched layouts produce "Module not found `_shared/<name>.ts`" at deploy time with no other hint. Canonical reference: any sibling under `supabase/functions/*/index.ts` paired with the deploy `files: [{name: "source/index.ts", ...}, {name: "_shared/jwt.ts", ...}, ...]` — `metrics-refresh` is the earliest example, `get-listing-detail` follows the same layout.
- **When a story's AC conflicts with `docs/tech-architecture.md`, the spec wins — but additive fields are defensible when the spec is under-specified.** Ralph rule #1 is "specs win," and that holds for response envelopes, DB column shape, and anything the spec is explicit about. But specs often leave ambiguity at the field level — e.g. §5.4's `failed_conditions: Array<{ metric; required; actual }>` elides `platform` while `min_followers` is ambiguous across TikTok/Instagram. Pattern: align the envelope shape to the spec, but enrich with additive fields when the UI can't disambiguate from spec alone. Document the addition in the edge function's header comment block so future iterations see the rationale. Ref: `get-listing-detail/index.ts` returning `failed_conditions: [{metric, platform, required, actual}]` as a documented extension.
- **Concurrency via version-id pin + FOR UPDATE re-read, not engine duplication in PL/pgSQL.** When an edge function needs to "re-evaluate eligibility against the NOW-current listing version" (spec §5.5) without duplicating the TS eligibility engine in SQL, the sanctioned split is: (1) edge fn reads `listings.current_version_id` and runs TS eligibility against it, then (2) calls an RPC that re-reads the same listing under `select ... for update`, compares the locked `current_version_id` to `p_expected_version_id`, and returns `LISTING_VERSION_CHANGED` on mismatch so the client re-opens the detail view. The row lock serializes this flow against `app_private.bump_listing_version` (which runs under its own row lock on the listings UPDATE). Invariant depends on the bump trigger producing a new `current_version_id` for every eligibility-affecting mutation — if a future trigger change lets a condition edit land without bumping, the RPC would silently commit against stale TS eligibility. Document the dependency in the RPC header. Ref: `supabase/migrations/20260417270000_us_041_apply_to_listing_rpc.sql` + `apply-to-listing/index.ts`.
- **Fail-closed applies to malformed data, not just missing data.** Spec §3d's fail-closed rule covers the "null actual metric" case (user hasn't been scraped), but the same principle extends to unreadable THRESHOLD values: a `gte` pre-condition with a NULL or non-numeric `numeric_threshold` must fail the check, not silently pass. Silently returning `null` from the single-condition evaluator for a malformed row is how a future mis-migrated condition flips a listing to "always eligible" without any alarm. Rule: every branch of an eligibility-or-permission evaluator that hits unreadable input returns a fail, never an early "skip." Schema CHECKs should prevent the malformed row from existing, but the evaluator is the last line of defense. Ref: `_shared/eligibility.ts` `evaluatePreCondition`.
- **Extract shared modules under `supabase/functions/_shared/` as soon as a second function needs the same logic.** US-039's eligibility engine was inline; US-041 needed the same engine for its server-side re-check. Pattern: extract to `_shared/<name>.ts` with a header comment naming the consumers and the spec sections it implements, have BOTH functions import it via `../_shared/<name>.ts`, and **redeploy every consumer function** in the same iteration — the deployed bundle of a prior function still contains the inlined copy, so a future redeploy of that function without the shared file would fail with `Module not found`. The `deploy_edge_function` `files` array must include every `_shared/*.ts` dependency, not just the new one. Ref: `_shared/eligibility.ts` shared by `get-listing-detail` (v4) + `apply-to-listing` (v2).
- **`KeyboardAvoidingView` is unreliable inside an RN `<Modal>` (and therefore inside our `BottomSheet`).** The Modal opens its own native window, so KAV's measured frame and the keyboard's reported frame disagree and the contents do not get pushed up. Replace KAV with a direct `Keyboard.addListener('keyboardWillShow' / 'keyboardWillHide')` (use `keyboardDidShow`/`keyboardDidHide` on Android) that drives `paddingBottom` on the inner `<View>` from `e.endCoordinates.height`. Remember that `BottomSheet` snap point must accommodate `keyboard_height (~336px iOS) + content_height` — for the apply sheet that meant raising `snapPoints` from `[0.7]` to `[0.85]`. Ref: `src/screens/listing-detail/ApplySheet.tsx`.
- **Defer `router.replace`/`router.push` after dismissing a `BottomSheet` by ~350ms** so the sheet's spring-out animation (`SPRING_SNAPPY` settles in ~300ms — see `src/design/motion.ts`) plays cleanly before the new screen mounts. Without the delay, the sheet slides down on top of the freshly-rendered destination, which looks broken. The same delay applies to any imperative navigation triggered from a Modal/BottomSheet success path. Ref: `app/(creator)/listing/[id].tsx` `onApplied`.
- **Run cheap checks before upstream oEmbed/scraper calls in edge functions; return `502 OEMBED_UNAVAILABLE` (not 500) when the upstream itself fails.** Order inside an endpoint that relies on a third-party URL validator: JWT → role → parse body → regex-classify URL → DB lookups → status/permission gates → affirmation/eligibility check → upstream oEmbed → RPC. This is both a cost optimization (don't burn TikTok rate-limit slots on requests that will 4xx regardless) and a DDoS-amplifier guard (an unauth POST must not be able to force an upstream fetch). Split the error codes: `500 SERVER_MISCONFIGURED` / `500 DB_ERROR` for our-fault failures, `502 OEMBED_UNAVAILABLE` for upstream unavailability — clients that auto-retry benefit because upstream outages are usually retryable, DB bugs rarely are. Document BOTH codes in the contract comment. Ref: `supabase/functions/submit-video/index.ts` oEmbed section + error envelope header.
- **Defensive null-guards on NOT NULL columns fetched via service-role client, when the row feeds a security-relevant gate.** The database schema can enforce NOT NULL, but a TypeScript read from a future mis-migrated row that returns `null` will silently propagate into downstream logic. When the value gates a security check (e.g. `application.listing_version_id` → the post-condition affirmation check; an empty `.eq('listing_version_id', null)` fetch returns zero rows → no affirmations required → gate bypassed), add an explicit `if (!row.field) { 500 DB_ERROR }`. One extra branch is cheaper than a future exploitable silent-pass. Ref: `supabase/functions/submit-video/index.ts` listing_version_id guard.
- **PostgREST embeds silently NULL joined columns when the target table's RLS filters the caller out — always audit every `.select('..., related!fk(col)')` embed against the related table's RLS policies.** Failure mode: the embed syntax succeeds, returns rows, and populates the parent columns; the embedded field just comes back as `null` or `[]` under RLS because PostgREST applies the policies per-row transparently. Highest-risk embeds on this project: (a) `users!lister_id(username)` where `users_self_select` is self-only — every cross-tenant handle read (creator viewing lister handle, lister viewing applicant handle) will null out; (b) `listing_versions!...(...)` where `listing_versions_read_if_listing_readable` gates on `listings.status='active' OR caller=lister` — any historical/closed listing version the caller doesn't own will null; (c) `listings!...(title)` where `listings_public_read` requires `status='active'` — same closed-listing nullout. Rule: before writing a PostgREST embed, check the target table's RLS; if the policy wouldn't return the specific row under the caller's role, replace the embed with a SECURITY DEFINER RPC that does its own ownership check. DO NOT widen the self-only policy to "return usernames if handle is public" — that leaks the full row shape; DEFINER + narrow column projection is the correct primitive. Ref: Codebase Pattern #40 (`submission_reuse_count`), Codebase Pattern #110 (`apply_to_listing_rpc`), and `public.list_my_applications()` from US-043.
- **SECURITY DEFINER "list-my-rows-with-foreign-context" RPC skeleton.** Contract: `returns table(<flat columns including the cross-tenant bits>)`, `language sql stable security definer set search_path = ''`, body is a straight `select ... from public.<owned_table> t left join public.<foreign_table> f on ... where t.<owner_col> = public.current_user_id() order by ...`. Grants: `revoke all ... from public`, `revoke all ... from anon`, `grant execute ... to authenticated`. Service-role is intentionally not granted — it already has BYPASSRLS and wouldn't use this shape. Why `stable`, not `volatile`: the function reads but doesn't write, so PostgREST can plan around it and query planner caches can reuse results within a statement. Why `sql` over `plpgsql`: the body is one statement, no control flow, no GET STACKED DIAGNOSTICS — inlines better. Verified pattern via a transactional `begin; set local role authenticated; set local "request.jwt.claims" = '{"sub":"<uuid>","role":"creator"}'; select * from public.<fn>();` block that confirms (a) the DEFINER bypass returns the cross-tenant columns, (b) the ownership check still gates other users' rows (swap the sub claim to a different creator and assert zero rows). Ref: `supabase/migrations/20260418010100_us_043_list_my_applications_rpc.sql`. Applies to any future "my X with lister/creator/reviewer context" list screen.
- **Screen-RPC naming convention: `public.list_my_<things>` for caller-scoped list reads, `public.<action>_rpc` for mutations, `public.<thing>_<metric>(arg)` for aggregates.** Precedents: `public.list_my_applications()` (US-043) / `public.apply_to_listing_rpc(...)` (US-041) / `public.submission_reuse_count(uuid)` (US-009). Keep the `list_my_*` functions argumentless (the caller is implicit via `current_user_id()`) — passing a caller UUID would re-introduce the spoofing surface the helpers exist to eliminate. Keep them in the `public` schema (not `app_private`) because they need to be callable via PostgREST `supabase.rpc(...)`; `app_private` is reserved for server-side triggers + cron bodies. The `authenticated` grant is mandatory — without it PostgREST returns 42501 "permission denied for function" and the MCP `execute_sql` test path (runs as `postgres`) silently passes, masking the bug.
- **Segmented control a11y: `accessibilityRole="tablist"` wrapper + `accessibilityRole="tab"` on each button + `accessibilityState={{ selected }}`**, NOT the Chip primitive's `role="checkbox"` + `checked`. Chip is for multi-select filter rows where each pill is independent; a segmented control is single-select and semantically is a tab bar. iOS VoiceOver reads "tab, Pending, selected, 1 of 4" for the tablist shape — correct under Apple's a11y guidance. `selected` is the right state (not `checked`) because the user isn't toggling a boolean, they're picking one of N mutually-exclusive views. Inline the control rather than promoting to a shared primitive until a second screen needs it — premature abstraction hurts more than a ~40-line duplicate. Ref: `SegmentControl` in `app/(creator)/applications.tsx` (US-043).
- **Enum-to-UI-bucket maps live as a static `Record<DBEnum, UISegment>` next to the screen that owns the mapping, not in a shared constants file.** `application_status` has 6 values; the Applied tab shows 4 segments (pending / approved / rejected / cancelled), with `withdrawn + cancelled_listing_edit + cancelled_listing_closed` all bucketing to "cancelled" per design.md §3.1 label + the AC's 4-segment design. Type the map as `Record<Database['public']['Enums']['application_status'], Segment>` so adding a 7th enum value trips tsc at the map literal. Do NOT collapse the three cancellation variants in the DB (they carry distinct semantic meaning for future admin/analytics use); collapse only at the UI layer. Same pattern applies to any status enum whose DB shape is richer than the screen's tab count. Ref: `STATUS_BUCKET` in `app/(creator)/applications.tsx`.
- **Two-phase verification probe pattern for screens whose populated case isn't reachable from the in-app DevRolePicker.** The DevRolePicker mints a literal `dev-creator-token` string that PostgREST rejects with PGRST301; only `auth-signup-creator`-issued JWTs are accepted. For screens that need populated-row screenshots (US-043 list-my-applications, future US-047 my-submissions), the project precedent is: (1) take empty-state mobile-mcp screenshots from the DevRolePicker session to validate layout + segment switching + each empty copy, (2) run a two-phase Bun probe that signs up a fresh creator (mode=signup → prints `{user_id, token}`), pauses for the operator to seed fixtures via Supabase MCP execute_sql, then (mode=query) calls the screen's RPC with the captured JWT and asserts the contract holds end-to-end through PostgREST. The probe **must mirror the screen's bucket-collapse map exactly** (typed `Record<EnumValue, Bucket>`, not a chained ternary with an implicit else) so a future enum addition trips tsc on both sides. Always delete the probe fixture (user + applications + cascading social_links/creator_profiles/metric_snapshots) via execute_sql immediately after the probe finishes; the user row is not auto-cascaded. Ref: `scripts/test/probe-us043-list-apps.ts`. Same posture US-036 took for the gesture-blocked refresh test.
- **Realtime `postgres_changes` without a server-side filter when the table lacks a direct caller-id column.** `applications` has `creator_id` so US-044's subscription filters on `creator_id=eq.<uuid>`; `submissions` has no such column (ownership runs through `submissions.application_id → applications.creator_id`). For tables gated via a join, OMIT the filter clause entirely and let the broker's RLS evaluation (`<table>_creator_rw` / `_lister_read` policies) gate visibility server-side. The alternative — passing `application_id=in.(...)` — would require the client to maintain the id set, re-subscribe on every new owned row, and race the RLS evaluation; strictly worse than letting RLS do its job. Keep the client-side row-match guard (`rowsRef.current.find((r) => r.id === next.id)`) so updates for rows that aren't currently surfaced by the RPC are ignored cheaply. Applies to any child-of-join table: `submission_videos`, future `review_threads`, etc. Ref: `app/(creator)/submissions.tsx` US-047 realtime effect.
- **Supabase Realtime tenant hibernation is an infrastructure-class blocker, not a code defect.** When `supabase.realtime.channel(...).subscribe()` returns `TIMED_OUT` repeatedly (4+ times) and `get_logs({service:'realtime'})` shows `UnableToConnectToProject: Realtime was unable to connect to the project database` every 5-15s, the project's Realtime tenant is stuck in an initialization loop that can stretch `Realtime.Tenants.Connect.CheckConnection` past 8 minutes before the broker gives up and the subscription never reaches `SUBSCRIBED`. The HTTP side (PostgREST, edge functions, `execute_sql`) stays healthy throughout — this is purely a realtime-broker-to-replication-slot connectivity issue. The `pg_stat_activity` view shows realtime's own connections as `idle`, giving a false-positive "it's connected" signal; trust the realtime logs' `UnableToConnectToProject` entries over `pg_stat_activity`. For realtime-dependent stories (US-044), the decision rule: if empty-state mobile-mcp screenshots + code-reviewer pass + RLS/publication membership verified at DB level, ship the implementation under `passes:false` with a detailed blocker note + ready-to-run probe, rather than looping indefinitely. Next iteration re-runs the probe once logs show `Starting stream replication for slot supabase_realtime_replication_slot_v2_83_2` (the postgres_changes slot, distinct from the `_messages_` slot that handles broadcast) without a trailing `UnableToConnectToProject`. Before investigating any realtime test failure, check these logs FIRST — client-side debugging is wasted effort when the broker can't reach Postgres. Ref: `scripts/test/probe-us044-realtime.ts` + this iteration's US-044 blocker notes.
- **Caller-scoped dashboard aggregate RPC beats N round-trips.** When a screen needs multiple `count(*)` aggregates all gated by the same caller-scoped filter (Lister Home: active campaigns / pending applications / pending submissions), expose ONE `SECURITY INVOKER` SQL function returning a typed row with all the counts rather than N PostgREST `.select('*', { count: 'exact', head: true })` calls. Three wins: (1) atomic — the numbers come from the same snapshot, no race where they disagree because they were fetched 20ms apart; (2) typed via `Database['public']['Functions']['<name>']['Returns'][number]` so the caller reads column names, not Range-header counts; (3) one network hop. Repeat the `<fkcol> = public.current_user_id()` filter inside EVERY sub-select even though RLS already applies — belt-and-suspenders so a future RLS regression doesn't silently inflate counts with other callers' rows. Pattern #42 still applies: `set search_path = ''` + schema-qualify enum casts (`'active'::public.listing_status`). Ref: `lister_dashboard_counts` in `us_048_lister_dashboard_counts_rpc.sql`.
- **`allowFontScaling={false}` vs `maxFontSizeMultiplier={1.3}` — they are NOT interchangeable.** Per docs/design.md §1.3 + §11 QA checklist: CONTENT text (body, captions, display-size numbers in stat tiles, card titles, anything informational) must honor system Dynamic Type up to 130% via `maxFontSizeMultiplier={1.3}`. UI CHROME (pills, chips, tab-bar labels, segment buttons — fixed-sized containers where scaling would break layout) opts out entirely with `allowFontScaling={false}`. The trap: a hero metric using `textStyles.display` LOOKS like chrome (big, standalone, inside a tile) but is CONTENT — the whole point of the screen. Getting this wrong makes the app inaccessible to users with large system text without any compile-time signal. Reviewer checklist: for every Text component, ask "does the meaning come from the value shown?" — if yes, `maxFontSizeMultiplier={1.3}`; if the text is a static UI label on a fixed-geometry element, `allowFontScaling={false}`. Ref: `StatTile` in `app/(lister)/dashboard.tsx`.
- **Zero-data state and empty-state are distinct UI states even when they share the same data shape.** A dashboard that renders `0 / 0 / 0` across three tiles when the user has nothing to show is a UX miss — design.md §5 specifies dedicated empty-state copy for every screen (Lister dashboard: "Post your first bounty."). The engineering rule: after a successful load, check if the data is semantically empty (all counts zero, empty list, etc.) and branch to the §5 copy BEFORE rendering the populated layout. Don't conflate "API returned successfully" with "there is data worth showing". Pattern applies to any counts-aggregate screen (creator earnings dashboard, lister analytics in v2, etc.) — each gets its own §5 empty-state copy, not a generic "No data." fallback. Ref: `isAllZero(counts)` branch in `app/(lister)/dashboard.tsx`.

- Files changed: `supabase/migrations/20260417080000_us_003_baseline_enums.sql`, `supabase/migrations/20260417080100_us_003_app_private_schema.sql`, `src/types/supabase.ts` (new; `.gitkeep` removed), `.env.example`, `.env` (local — gitignored)
- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors ✓ (zero findings) / mobile-mcp n/a (DB-only story)
- Code-reviewer verdict: issues fixed in this commit (added `app_private` schema migration; clarified pg_cron comment)
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - Reviewer flagged missing `app_private` schema — §4.7's extension block includes schema + revoke. Future baseline migrations should follow §4.7 ordering even when the immediate story doesn't need the schema yet.
  - Security advisor WARNs on any extension in `public`; always pin to `extensions` schema except for `pg_cron`.
  - `apply_migration` stores the SQL body verbatim; editing the local `.sql` file afterwards drifts from the migrations table. If you must change DB state, add another migration.
---

## 2026-04-17 09:05 — US-004
- Applied `us_004_identity_tables` via Supabase MCP creating `public.users`, `public.creator_profiles` (all 9 denorm metric columns), `public.lister_profiles`, `public.social_links` (with the two partial unique indexes on `(user_id, platform)` and `(platform, handle)` scoped to `status <> 'unlinked'`).
- Regenerated `src/types/supabase.ts` — the four new tables appear under `Database["public"]["Tables"]`; enums unchanged from US-003.
- Files changed: `supabase/migrations/20260417090000_us_004_identity_tables.sql` (new), `src/types/supabase.ts`
- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors ✓ (only INFO `rls_enabled_no_policy` — deferred to US-009 per story) / mobile-mcp n/a (DB-only story)
- Code-reviewer verdict: approved (no medium+ findings; field-for-field match against §4.7 lines 481–562)
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - Qualify `citext` as `extensions.citext` — default migration search_path does not include `extensions`.
  - Supabase auto-enables RLS on new `public.*` tables on this project; advisors show INFO `rls_enabled_no_policy` (not `rls_disabled`) until policies land in US-009. Harmless until then.
  - The spec's `create index on public.tbl (...)` (anonymous) is fine to keep verbatim — later migrations referring back to these indexes can use `pg_indexes` or rename at that point if needed.

## 2026-04-17 07:45 — US-002
- Installed 13 core runtime deps via `bunx expo install` (Supabase JS, AsyncStorage, url-polyfill, expo-router, Reanimated, gesture-handler, svg, lucide, haptics, linking, constants, font, mmkv); also added `react-native-worklets` explicitly since babel references `react-native-worklets/plugin`.
- Configured `babel.config.js` with `react-native-worklets/plugin`; flipped `package.json#main` to `expo-router/entry`; added minimal `app/_layout.tsx` (GestureHandlerRootView + Slot) and `app/index.tsx`; deleted orphaned `App.tsx` + `index.ts`.
- Files changed: `package.json`, `bun.lock`, `app.json` (auto-added expo-router/expo-font config plugins), `babel.config.js`, `app/_layout.tsx`, `app/index.tsx`, `App.tsx` (deleted), `index.ts` (deleted), `scripts/ralph/screenshots/us-002-boot.png`
- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors n/a / mobile-mcp ✓ (booted iPhone 17 Pro sim, Expo Go loaded bundle, "Marketify" renders on cream background — see `scripts/ralph/screenshots/us-002-boot.png`)
- Code-reviewer verdict: issues fixed in this commit (added `react-native-worklets` explicit dep)
- Spec gap: story title mentions NativeWind but the explicit install list does not include it; specs (design.md, product-plan.md, tech-architecture.md) don't mandate NativeWind either. Skipping for now — design tokens (US-022) + StyleSheet is a viable path. Human to confirm.
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - Expo Go cannot load screens that require the `<Stack>` navigator from expo-router 6 under new-arch — use `<Slot />` or a dev-client build
  - `bunx expo install <pkg>` pins SDK-compatible versions; it just downgraded transitive worklets 0.8.1 → 0.5.1 to match SDK 54. Trust it.
  - `bunx expo install expo-router` auto-appends `"plugins": ["expo-router", ...]` to `app.json`
  - mobile-mcp's `mobile_open_url` refuses non-http(s) schemes; use `xcrun simctl openurl <udid> exp://...` to deep-link into Expo Go
---

## 2026-04-17 10:30 — US-005
- Applied `us_005_listing_tables` via Supabase MCP: `public.listings` (20 cols incl. plain-integer `min_followers_tiktok`/`_instagram` with partial btree indexes on `status='active'`), `public.listing_versions` with `(listing_id, version_number)` unique, self-FK `previous_version_id`, and the circular FK `listings.current_version_id → listing_versions.id` closed via a post-create `alter table`.
- Added `public.listing_conditions` (data-driven pre+post rows discriminated by `condition_kind` enum per spec §4.7 block 8, not separate `_pre_`/`_post_` tables as the story text suggested) and `public.sample_videos` (§4.7 block 9, same version-scoped grouping).
- Files changed: `supabase/migrations/20260417100000_us_005_listing_tables.sql` (new), `src/types/supabase.ts`
- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors ✓ (only INFO `rls_enabled_no_policy` on the 4 new tables — expected; policies land in US-009) / mobile-mcp n/a (DB-only story)
- Code-reviewer verdict: approved (one cosmetic note on explicit vs anonymous index naming — no functional impact, not fixed)
- Spec gap: Story acceptance said `listing_pre_conditions` + `listing_post_conditions`; spec §4.7 block 8 defines a single `listing_conditions` table discriminated by the `condition_kind` enum. Followed the spec. Human to acknowledge if we actually wanted split tables — unlikely given the cascade trigger and eligibility engine both read from the single table.
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - When a story's table-naming contradicts the spec DDL, the spec wins (per Ralph instructions) — document as a Spec gap in the migration header + progress.txt.
  - `sample_videos` belongs with listing_versions (version-scoped FK); bundle it with the listing-tables migration rather than deferring — cascade trigger in US-010 treats "sample videos" as a versioned field.
  - Anonymous `create index on public.tbl (...)` in the spec is a style choice, not a requirement. Explicit names are fine and arguably better for later reindex operations; the reviewer flagged it as a benign deviation.

- Applied `us_006_pipeline_tables` via Supabase MCP: `public.applications` (10 cols, 3 indexes incl. partial-unique `applications_open_uniq` on `(listing_id, creator_id) where status in ('pending','approved')`), `public.submissions` (11 cols incl. reserved override audit trail + `submissions_override_requires_approved` check), `public.submission_videos`, and `public.submission_reuse_view` for the ReuseBadge aggregate.
- Follow-up migration `us_006_submission_reuse_view_invoker` flips the view to `security_invoker=on` to clear the advisor ERROR. Reviewer flagged that this interacts with US-009 RLS — promoted to Codebase Patterns as a US-009 handoff.
- Files changed: `supabase/migrations/20260417110000_us_006_pipeline_tables.sql` (new), `supabase/migrations/20260417110100_us_006_submission_reuse_view_invoker.sql` (new), `src/types/supabase.ts`
- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors ✓ (zero ERROR-level after the security_invoker fix; only INFO `rls_enabled_no_policy` on the 3 new tables — expected, policies land in US-009) / mobile-mcp n/a (DB-only story)
- Code-reviewer verdict: approved with one US-009 handoff (see Codebase Patterns). Byte-for-byte match on DDL vs. spec §4.7 blocks 10-11.
- Spec gap: Story AC mentions "the reserved revision-request column on submissions" but spec explicitly states "No `revision_requested` in v1" (§2.2 line 121, §14.2 line 2014). The reserved columns per spec are `override_by_user_id` + `override_reason` (override audit trail, design §4.6 OverrideEligibilityDialog). Followed the spec; documented in the migration header.
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - Default Supabase views trip the `security_definer_view` advisor — always add `alter view <name> set (security_invoker = on)` in the same migration group.
  - When a spec contradicts itself (inline DDL comment vs. global standard), follow the global standard and flag the local contradiction as a future-story handoff rather than trying to reconcile in the current story.

content.content:
1	import { Redirect, Slot } from 'expo-router';
2	import { useAuth } from '@/lib/auth';
3	import { WizardProvider } from '@/screens/campaign-wizard/WizardStore';
4	
5	// Campaign-creation wizard route group (US-049 through US-053). Wraps the
6	// step-X screens in a single `WizardProvider` so draft state persists across
7	// navigations without re-mounting the store. Gated to the lister role — the
8	// parent `(lister)` layout already enforces this, but re-check here keeps the
9	// wizard robust to future route-group flattening.
10	//
11	// Spec gap: US-049's AC names the screen path `app/(lister)/create/step-1.tsx`,
12	// but the already-wired FAB (US-048) routes to `/(lister)/campaigns/new/step-1`
13	// and the canonical lister nav tree puts campaign-scoped flows under
14	// `/(lister)/campaigns/*` per docs/design.md §3.2 + §2.3. Using
15	// `campaigns/new/*` keeps the FAB functional and the URL shape consistent
16	// without retroactively re-wiring US-048.
17	
18	export default function CampaignWizardLayout() {
19	  const { role } = useAuth();
20	  if (role !== 'lister') return <Redirect href="/(auth)" />;
21	
22	  return (
23	    <WizardProvider>
24	      <Slot />
25	    </WizardProvider>
26	  );
27	}
28

28-
29-| Role | Token | Hex | Usage |
30-|---|---|---|---|
31-| Primary | `--primary` | `#EC4899` | Brand, active tab icon, primary surfaces, logo mark |
32-| Primary Soft | `--primary-soft` | `#FDF2F8` | Primary-tinted backgrounds, selected chip bg |
33-| Primary Deep | `--primary-deep` | `#831843` | Primary text on soft bg, pressed states |
34:| Accent (CTA) | `--cta` | `#F97316` | All primary action buttons, "Apply", "Submit", "Create Campaign" |
35-| Accent Deep | `--cta-deep` | `#9A3412` | CTA pressed state |
36-| Ink (text) | `--ink` | `#0F172A` | All body and heading text, hard borders |
37-| Ink-70 | `--ink-70` | `#475569` | Secondary text, captions |
38-| Ink-40 | `--ink-40` | `#94A3B8` | Tertiary / disabled text |
39-| Surface | `--surface` | `#FFFFFF` | Card bodies |
40-| Canvas | `--canvas` | `#FFF9F2` | App background (warm off-white — matches orange CTA undertone) |
--
158-| Notifications | `/(creator)/notifications` | Activity inbox | Tap item |
159-
160-### 2.3 Lister Screens
161-
162-| Screen | Route | Purpose | Primary CTA |
163-|---|---|---|---|
164:| Dashboard | `/(lister)/dashboard` | Stat tiles + recent activity | "Create campaign" (FAB) |
165:| Create Campaign — Steps 1–5 | `/(lister)/campaigns/new/*` | Wizard: basics → price → pre → post → samples → review | "Next" / "Publish" |
166-| My Campaigns | `/(lister)/campaigns` | List active + inactive | Tap card |
167-| Campaign Detail (lister) | `/(lister)/campaigns/[id]` | Manage: toggle active, edit, counts | "Edit" / Toggle |
168-| Edit Campaign | `/(lister)/campaigns/[id]/edit` | Modify fields (triggers cancel-warning) | "Save changes" |
169-| Applications Inbox | `/(lister)/campaigns/[id]/applications` | Review applicants with metrics | Tap row |
170-| Application Review | `/(lister)/campaigns/[id]/applications/[appId]` | Approve/Reject | "Approve" / "Reject" |
171-| Submissions Inbox | `/(lister)/campaigns/[id]/submissions` | Review final videos | Tap row |
--
229-
230-**Inbox model — relationship to §2.3 IA.** §2.3 lists Applications Inbox and Submissions Inbox as *campaign-scoped* routes (`/(lister)/campaigns/[id]/applications` and `/(lister)/campaigns/[id]/submissions`) because review always happens in-context for a specific campaign. The **Inbox tab** (`/(lister)/inbox`) is a cross-campaign roll-up — same underlying data, different entry point:
231-
232-- **Segmented header:** `Applications · Submissions` (segmented control, not two tabs — keeps the 4-tab bar).
233-- **Default segment:** whichever has the higher unreviewed count; ties go to Submissions (higher creator-anxiety cost per day of delay).
234-- **Row grouping:** grouped by campaign with `SectionHeader` rows; tapping a row pushes into the campaign-scoped review screen (same route as §2.3).
235:- **Empty state:** if both segments empty, "Nothing to review. Good time to post another bounty." with a CTA button to Create Campaign.
236-
237-So the campaign-scoped routes are the sources of truth; the Inbox tab is a lens. This matches how lister work actually happens — sometimes you think "that Nike campaign has applicants" (campaign-first), sometimes "do I have anything to review?" (inbox-first).
238-
239-### 3.3 Modal vs Stack vs Tab
240-
241-| Case | Pattern | Why |
242-|---|---|---|
243-| Filters (creator feed) | Bottom sheet (80% height) | Scannable, preserves feed scroll. |
244-| Apply Confirm | Full-screen modal slide-up | Commitment moment — needs focus. |
245:| Create Campaign wizard | Stack with progress header | 5 steps need back-navigation. |
246-| Edit Campaign | Full-screen modal | Single-page edit. |
247-| Application Review (lister) | Stack push | Back-swipe preserved. |
248-| Submission Review (lister) | Stack push with video | Back-swipe preserved. |
249-| Submission Composer | Stack push | Mid-flow from application detail. |
250-| Confirmations | Alert-style modal | Short, high-stakes. |
251-| Notifications | Stack push from bell | Allows deep-link. |
--
512-│ [    SEND FOR REVIEW    ]                 │
513-└───────────────────────────────────────────┘
514-```
515-
516-**States:** Empty URL (dashed placeholder), Invalid (danger border + helper), Fetching (shimmer), Preview failed (non-blocking), Submitting (spinner), Success (full-screen card, auto-dismiss).

└───────────────────────────────────────────┘

input.prompt:
Review the implementation of US-050 in this Marketify repo (Expo + React Native + Supabase).

**Story US-050 — Create-campaign wizard step 2 — pre-conditions**

Acceptance criteria (verbatim from scripts/ralph/prd.json):
- step-2 screen lets the lister add rows: platform (TikTok/IG), metric (followers, avg_views), operator (>=), threshold
- Saves to wizard store
- Mobile-mcp verification: add two rows, screenshot (this is done separately — not part of your scope)
- Commit

**What to review**

Only the new file in this diff:
- `app/(lister)/campaigns/new/step-2.tsx` (new, ~320 lines)

That file imports from the pre-existing `src/screens/campaign-wizard/WizardStore.tsx` (which already defines `WizardPreCondition`, `WizardPreConditionPlatform`, `WizardPreConditionMetric`, `setField`). Do not rewrite the store — it was built in US-049 and is shared across all five steps.

Sibling to read for convention: `app/(lister)/campaigns/new/step-1.tsx` (reference implementation for the header/back/Next pattern).

**Spec references**
- docs/design.md §4.5 Create Campaign Wizard (Lister) — shows the grouped-by-platform layout, but the US-050 AC explicitly says "add rows: platform + metric + threshold" (per-row platform choice, not platform-grouped). Per Ralph conventions when AC and spec conflict at the UI layer, an additive interpretation is acceptable (see Codebase Pattern #109 in scripts/ralph/progress.txt).
- docs/tech-architecture.md §4.7 block 8 (`listing_conditions`): `operator` is `gte/lte/eq/contains/bool`; for this step we hard-code `gte` since the AC says ">=". The wizard store holds UI-friendly metric names (`followers`, `avg_views`); the DB enum (`min_followers`, `min_avg_views_last_n`) mapping will happen at step-5 publish.

**Focus areas (confidence ≥ 80 only)**
1. Correctness against AC: does the screen actually let the user add rows, edit platform/metric/threshold, and remove rows? Does it persist to the wizard store?
2. TypeScript: no `any`, consistent with the codebase. Hook dependency arrays correct.
3. Accessibility: labels, roles, allowFontScaling rules (UI chrome vs content — see Codebase Pattern #126).
4. Navigation: `router.push('/(lister)/campaigns/new/step-3')` targets a path that doesn't exist yet (step-3 lands in US-051). Is that acceptable per the wizard contract, or does it need a guard?
5. Data hygiene: threshold is stored as `number`; is `0` the right "unset" sentinel, or should it be `number | null`? Note that the wizard store types `threshold: number` (non-null) so changing that is a store change.
6. Neubrutalist styling: Chip + shadows.hard usage follows Codebase Patterns #77, #81.

**Out of scope** — don't comment on:
- Missing step-3 (it's a separate story)
- Mobile-mcp verification (separate step)
- The fact that there's no `submit to DB` path (step-5's job)
- The `void Plus` / dead import — it has been removed already

Report: a list of findings, each with a confidence score (0-100) and severity (CRITICAL / IMPORTANT / MINOR / NIT). Only surface findings at confidence ≥ 80. No summary prose — just the findings list.

content.text:
I now have everything I need. Here are the findings:

---

**Reviewing:** `app/(lister)/campaigns/new/step-2.tsx` (new file, US-050)

---

**IMPORTANT**

**1. `onAddRow` / `onRemoveRow` / `onUpdateRow` capture stale `draft.preConditions` (confidence: 90)**

File: `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/app/(lister)/campaigns/new/step-2.tsx`, lines 62–90

All three mutation callbacks depend on `draft.preConditions` in their closure and list it as a `useCallback` dependency. `setField` in the WizardStore (line 152–160 of WizardStore.tsx) accepts the **new value directly**, not a functional updater. This means: if the user taps "Add condition" twice in rapid succession before a re-render, the second `onAddRow` fires with the same `draft.preConditions` snapshot it closed over (the pre-first-add array), and the first added row is silently dropped. The same race applies to `onUpdateRow` and `onRemoveRow`. The WizardStore's `setField` calls `setDraft((prev) => ...)` with a functional updater internally, but `onAddRow` has already captured `draft.preConditions` before calling `setField` — the stale read is at the call site, not inside the store.

Fix: use `setPartial` with a functional form, or refactor the store to accept a functional updater overload for arrays. The minimum surgical fix without store changes: read `draft.preConditions` inside the callback body from a `useRef` that mirrors the latest draft, or accept the race as low-risk for a wizard (single-user, sequential taps) and document it. Given that the wizard specifically encourages adding multiple rows, this is a real-world-hittable bug.

---

**2. Content `Text` nodes missing `maxFontSizeMultiplier={1.3}` (confidence: 85)**

File: `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/app/(lister)/campaigns/new/step-2.tsx`, lines 125–128, 142–144, 197, 210, 226

`docs/design.md` §8 checklist (line 769) states: "All content text respects system Dynamic Type / font scale up to 130% (`maxFontSizeMultiplier = 1.3`); UI chrome (pills, tab labels, status chips) opts out with `allowFontScaling={false}`." Codebase Pattern #126 (promoted from US-048) explicitly calls out the cutline: content text gets `maxFontSizeMultiplier={1.3}`, UI chrome gets `allowFontScaling={false}`.

The file correctly uses `allowFontScaling={false}` on the step label (line 122) and the `>=` operator badge (line 243) — those are UI chrome. However, the following content `Text` nodes have neither prop and will scale unboundedly:

- "Who can apply?" title (`textStyles.display`, line 125)
- Subtitle body text (line 127–129)
- "No rules yet. Add one below." empty hint (line 142–144)
- "Rule N" card title (`textStyles.caption`, line 197)
- "Platform" and "Metric" group labels (lines 210, 226)

`step-1.tsx` (the reference implementation) similarly omits `maxFontSizeMultiplier` on its content text — however the project spec and the explicitly promoted Codebase Pattern both require it. The issue exists in the reference file too, but the review scope is step-2.tsx and the pattern is a confirmed project rule.

Fix: add `maxFontSizeMultiplier={1.3}` to the content `Text` nodes listed above.

---

**3. `Chip` `onPress` uses an inline arrow function, bypassing stable callback identity (confidence: 82)**

File: `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/app/(lister)/campaigns/new/step-2.tsx`, lines 216–218, 231–233

Inside `ConditionCard`, the platform and metric `Chip` elements receive `onPress={() => onPlatformPick(p.value)}` and `onPress={() => onMetricPick(m.value)}`. `onPlatformPick` and `onMetricPick` are already memoized with `useCallback` (lines 174–180), but wrapping them in a new inline arrow function at render time defeats that memoization — a new function reference is created on every render of `ConditionCard`. `Chip` uses `onPress` in its own `useCallback` dependency array (`handlePressIn` / `handlePressOut` in `Chip.tsx` don't depend on `onPress`, so there's no cascading re-subscription today). However the more important concern is correctness against Codebase Pattern #77: "Every primitive that needs a press animation … should reuse this shape" — Chip's `onPress` is passed to `Pressable` directly and called only on the tap event, so the inline arrow is not a behavior bug in isolation. The confidence here is bounded because the Chip's press path doesn't close over `onPress` in a way that would snapshot a stale value. Rating this IMPORTANT rather than CRITICAL because it is a pattern inconsistency and a latent perf issue, but not an observable functional bug today.

Fix: either inline the chip rows with precomputed per-platform/per-metric callbacks (e.g., `onTikTokPress` / `onIGPress`), or accept the pattern as consistent with how `step-1.tsx` wires currency chips.

- docs/design.md §4.5 Create Campaign Wizard (Lister) — shows the grouped-by-platform layout, but the US-050 AC explicitly says "add rows: platform + metric + threshold" (per-row platform choice, not platform-grouped). Per Ralph conventions when AC and spec conflict at the UI layer, an additive interpretation is acceptable (see Codebase Pattern #109 in scripts/ralph/progress.txt).
- docs/tech-architecture.md §4.7 block 8 (`listing_conditions`): `operator` is `gte/lte/eq/contains/bool`; for this step we hard-code `gte` since the AC says ">=". The wizard store holds UI-friendly metric names (`followers`, `avg_views`); the DB enum (`min_followers`, `min_avg_views_last_n`) mapping will happen at step-5 publish.

**Focus areas (confidence ≥ 80 only)**
1. Correctness against AC: does the screen actually let the user add rows, edit platform/metric/threshold, and remove rows? Does it persist to the wizard store?
2. TypeScript: no `any`, consistent with the codebase. Hook dependency arrays correct.
3. Accessibility: labels, roles, allowFontScaling rules (UI chrome vs content — see Codebase Pattern #126).
4. Navigation: `router.push('/(lister)/campaigns/new/step-3')` targets a path that doesn't exist yet (step-3 lands in US-051). Is that acceptable per the wizard contract, or does it need a guard?
5. Data hygiene: threshold is stored as `number`; is `0` the right "unset" sentinel, or should it be `number | null`? Note that the wizard store types `threshold: number` (non-null) so changing that is a store change.
6. Neubrutalist styling: Chip + shadows.hard usage follows Codebase Patterns #77, #81.

**Out of scope** — don't comment on:
- Missing step-3 (it's a separate story)
- Mobile-mcp verification (separate step)
- The fact that there's no `submit to DB` path (step-5's job)
- The `void Plus` / dead import — it has been removed already

Report: a list of findings, each with a confidence score (0-100) and severity (CRITICAL / IMPORTANT / MINOR / NIT). Only surface findings at confidence ≥ 80. No summary prose — just the findings list.

content.text:
I now have everything I need. Here are the findings:

---

**Reviewing:** `app/(lister)/campaigns/new/step-2.tsx` (new file, US-050)

---

**IMPORTANT**

**1. `onAddRow` / `onRemoveRow` / `onUpdateRow` capture stale `draft.preConditions` (confidence: 90)**

File: `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/app/(lister)/campaigns/new/step-2.tsx`, lines 62–90

All three mutation callbacks depend on `draft.preConditions` in their closure and list it as a `useCallback` dependency. `setField` in the WizardStore (line 152–160 of WizardStore.tsx) accepts the **new value directly**, not a functional updater. This means: if the user taps "Add condition" twice in rapid succession before a re-render, the second `onAddRow` fires with the same `draft.preConditions` snapshot it closed over (the pre-first-add array), and the first added row is silently dropped. The same race applies to `onUpdateRow` and `onRemoveRow`. The WizardStore's `setField` calls `setDraft((prev) => ...)` with a functional updater internally, but `onAddRow` has already captured `draft.preConditions` before calling `setField` — the stale read is at the call site, not inside the store.

Fix: use `setPartial` with a functional form, or refactor the store to accept a functional updater overload for arrays. The minimum surgical fix without store changes: read `draft.preConditions` inside the callback body from a `useRef` that mirrors the latest draft, or accept the race as low-risk for a wizard (single-user, sequential taps) and document it. Given that the wizard specifically encourages adding multiple rows, this is a real-world-hittable bug.

---

**2. Content `Text` nodes missing `maxFontSizeMultiplier={1.3}` (confidence: 85)**

File: `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/app/(lister)/campaigns/new/step-2.tsx`, lines 125–128, 142–144, 197, 210, 226

`docs/design.md` §8 checklist (line 769) states: "All content text respects system Dynamic Type / font scale up to 130% (`maxFontSizeMultiplier = 1.3`); UI chrome (pills, tab labels, status chips) opts out with `allowFontScaling={false}`." Codebase Pattern #126 (promoted from US-048) explicitly calls out the cutline: content text gets `maxFontSizeMultiplier={1.3}`, UI chrome gets `allowFontScaling={false}`.

The file correctly uses `allowFontScaling={false}` on the step label (line 122) and the `>=` operator badge (line 243) — those are UI chrome. However, the following content `Text` nodes have neither prop and will scale unboundedly:

- "Who can apply?" title (`textStyles.display`, line 125)
- Subtitle body text (line 127–129)
- "No rules yet. Add one below." empty hint (line 142–144)
- "Rule N" card title (`textStyles.caption`, line 197)
- "Platform" and "Metric" group labels (lines 210, 226)

`step-1.tsx` (the reference implementation) similarly omits `maxFontSizeMultiplier` on its content text — however the project spec and the explicitly promoted Codebase Pattern both require it. The issue exists in the reference file too, but the review scope is step-2.tsx and the pattern is a confirmed project rule.

Fix: add `maxFontSizeMultiplier={1.3}` to the content `Text` nodes listed above.

---

**3. `Chip` `onPress` uses an inline arrow function, bypassing stable callback identity (confidence: 82)**

File: `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/app/(lister)/campaigns/new/step-2.tsx`, lines 216–218, 231–233

Inside `ConditionCard`, the platform and metric `Chip` elements receive `onPress={() => onPlatformPick(p.value)}` and `onPress={() => onMetricPick(m.value)}`. `onPlatformPick` and `onMetricPick` are already memoized with `useCallback` (lines 174–180), but wrapping them in a new inline arrow function at render time defeats that memoization — a new function reference is created on every render of `ConditionCard`. `Chip` uses `onPress` in its own `useCallback` dependency array (`handlePressIn` / `handlePressOut` in `Chip.tsx` don't depend on `onPress`, so there's no cascading re-subscription today). However the more important concern is correctness against Codebase Pattern #77: "Every primitive that needs a press animation … should reuse this shape" — Chip's `onPress` is passed to `Pressable` directly and called only on the tap event, so the inline arrow is not a behavior bug in isolation. The confidence here is bounded because the Chip's press path doesn't close over `onPress` in a way that would snapshot a stale value. Rating this IMPORTANT rather than CRITICAL because it is a pattern inconsistency and a latent perf issue, but not an observable functional bug today.

Fix: either inline the chip rows with precomputed per-platform/per-metric callbacks (e.g., `onTikTokPress` / `onIGPress`), or accept the pattern as consistent with how `step-1.tsx` wires currency chips.

Reviewer found 2 important issues to fix (stale closure in row mutations, and missing `maxFontSizeMultiplier` on content text). Finding #3 (inline arrows in Chip onPress) matches the sibling step-1 pattern and is not an observable bug — acceptable per Codebase Pattern around surgical changes.

Fix #1 first: extend `WizardStore` with a functional-updater variant.

content.content:
127	- **Zero-data state and empty-state are distinct UI states even when they share the same data shape.** A dashboard that renders `0 / 0 / 0` across three tiles when the user has nothing to show is a UX miss — design.md §5 specifies dedicated empty-state copy for every screen (Lister dashboard: "Post your first bounty."). The engineering rule: after a successful load, check if the data is semantically empty (all counts zero, empty list, etc.) and branch to the §5 copy BEFORE rendering the populated layout. Don't conflate "API returned successfully" with "there is data worth showing". Pattern applies to any counts-aggregate screen (creator earnings dashboard, lister analytics in v2, etc.) — each gets its own §5 empty-state copy, not a generic "No data." fallback. Ref: `isAllZero(counts)` branch in `app/(lister)/dashboard.tsx`.
128	
129	---
130	
131	## 2026-04-18 02:45 — US-043 verification close-out
132	- JWT drift was resolved upstream by US-036's claim-structure fix (auth.jwt() ->> 'app_role'); probe-jwt-drift.ts now returns 200 on PostgREST. Re-ran end-to-end mobile + HTTP verification to close US-043 cleanly without any code/migration changes.

- **Zero-data state and empty-state are distinct UI states even when they share the same data shape.** A dashboard that renders `0 / 0 / 0` across three tiles when the user has nothing to show is a UX miss — design.md §5 specifies dedicated empty-state copy for every screen (Lister dashboard: "Post your first bounty."). The engineering rule: after a successful load, check if the data is semantically empty (all counts zero, empty list, etc.) and branch to the §5 copy BEFORE rendering the populated layout. Don't conflate "API returned successfully" with "there is data worth showing". Pattern applies to any counts-aggregate screen (creator earnings dashboard, lister analytics in v2, etc.) — each gets its own §5 empty-state copy, not a generic "No data." fallback. input.new_string:
- **Zero-data state and empty-state are distinct UI states even when they share the same data shape.** A dashboard that renders `0 / 0 / 0` across three tiles when the user has nothing to show is a UX miss — design.md §5 specifies dedicated empty-state copy for every screen (Lister dashboard: "Post your first bounty."). The engineering rule: after a successful load, check if the data is semantically empty (all counts zero, empty list, etc.) and branch to the §5 copy BEFORE rendering the populated layout. Don't conflate "API returned successfully" with "there is data worth showing". Pattern applies to any counts-aggregate screen (creator earnings dashboard, lister analytics in v2, etc.) — each gets its own §5 empty-state copy, not a generic "No data." fallback. Ref: `isAllZero(counts)` branch in `app/(lister)/dashboard.tsx`.
- **Functional-updater context methods for state whose next value depends on the previous.** A plain `setField(key, value)` API forces callers to compute `value` from a closure-captured snapshot (`[...draft.preConditions, row]`), which stale-closes on rapid-fire handlers (two taps in one render tick both see the pre-first-tap array). The fix at the context level is an `updateField(key, (prev) => next)` variant that threads the functional updater all the way down to React's `setState` — then the callback reads the latest value at commit time, not at bind time. Every wizard-like store (WizardStore, future onboarding store) should expose both shapes: `setField` for scalar writes where prev is irrelevant, `updateField` for arrays / counters / anything derived. The pattern complements Codebase Pattern #106 (synchronous ref locks for the tap side); updateField handles the state side, the ref lock handles the tap-dedup side, and using them together is belt-and-suspenders. Ref: `src/screens/campaign-wizard/WizardStore.tsx` `updateField` (US-050).

---

## 2026-04-18 07:10 — US-050
- New screen `app/(lister)/campaigns/new/step-2.tsx` (~280 lines): header "Step 2 of 5 / Who can apply?" + stacked condition cards (each: platform chips TikTok/Instagram, metric chips Followers/Avg views, fixed `≥` operator badge, numeric threshold input, Remove icon button) + "Add condition" secondary CTA + "Next" primary CTA. Pre-conditions are optional — empty list is a valid "accept anyone" listing, Next is always enabled.
- Extended `src/screens/campaign-wizard/WizardStore.tsx` with `updateField<K>(key, (prev) => next)` alongside the existing `setField(key, value)`. Step-2's add/remove/update-row callbacks all use `updateField` so the functional updater reads the latest array at React commit time, not at closure-bind time — addresses code-reviewer finding #1 (confidence 90) about rapid-fire taps dropping writes.
- Added `maxFontSizeMultiplier={1.3}` to all content `Text` nodes on step-2 per Codebase Pattern #126 (title, subtitle, empty hint, "Rule N" card title, Platform/Metric group labels). UI chrome on the card (the `≥` operator badge, step label) keeps `allowFontScaling={false}`. Addresses code-reviewer finding #2 (confidence 85).
- Files changed:
  - `app/(lister)/campaigns/new/step-2.tsx` (new)
  - `src/screens/campaign-wizard/WizardStore.tsx` (updateField added; setPartial unchanged)
  - `scripts/ralph/screenshots/us-050-empty.png` (new)
  - `scripts/ralph/screenshots/us-050-two-rows.png` (new)
  - `scripts/ralph/prd.json` (US-050 passes:true + notes)
  - `scripts/ralph/progress.txt` (this entry + new Codebase Pattern)
- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors n/a (UI-only story, no DDL) / mobile-mcp ✓ (iPhone 17 Pro sim iOS 26.3 — Expo Go — dev-signin-lister → FAB → step-1 restored from AsyncStorage draft → Next → step-2 empty-state render → Add condition x2 → Row 1 set to TikTok/Followers/10000, Row 2 set to Instagram/Avg views/5000, both rows persist independently, Add+Next CTAs remain visible + enabled, screenshots captured).
- Code-reviewer verdict: issues fixed in this commit. IMPORTANT (confidence 90): stale-closure in row mutation callbacks — fixed by adding `updateField` functional-updater to the store and rewiring all three callbacks. IMPORTANT (confidence 85): content Text nodes missing `maxFontSizeMultiplier={1.3}` per Codebase Pattern #126 — fixed on all six content nodes. IMPORTANT (confidence 82): inline arrow functions in Chip `onPress` defeating `useCallback` memoization — accepted as-is because (a) the reviewer explicitly rated this as "pattern inconsistency and latent perf issue, but not an observable functional bug today", (b) it matches the sibling step-1 pattern, and (c) the per-card array is bounded at 2 platforms + 2 metrics so the cost is negligible. Promoting to a shared primitive would be premature abstraction.
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - **When a context exposes `setField(key, value)`, audit every call site that derives `value` from the current state — those are latent stale-closures.** Symptom: rapid-fire handlers (tap-twice before re-render) silently drop writes. Fix: thread a functional-updater `updateField(key, prev => next)` through the context. Same pattern as React's `setState(fn)` vs `setState(value)`. Promoted to Codebase Patterns.
  - **The WizardStore's persistence path (`schedulePersist` inside the functional setDraft) works correctly with `updateField` because `schedulePersist` always sees the freshly-computed `next` object.** No separate persistence fix needed — the 250ms debounce continues to coalesce multiple rapid updates, and the unmount-flush path in WizardStore's cleanup effect still reads from `draftRef.current` which is always the latest. Adding updateField is a pure additive change to the public API.
  - **US-044 remains infra-blocked this iteration** — realtime logs still show only the `_messages_` replication slot starting, no `supabase_realtime_replication_slot_v2_83_2` (postgres_changes), and `UnableToConnectToProject` errors continue. Per the established pattern (commits 11f377d, 47b51c3, 4th re-verify attempt), skipped the probe to avoid loop-indefinitely thrash and moved forward to US-050. Next iteration should re-check realtime logs first; if the postgres_changes slot starts cleanly, run `bun run scripts/test/probe-us044-realtime.ts` and close US-044.

lines: - **Caller-scoped dashboard aggregate RPC beats N round-trips.** When a screen needs multiple `count(*)` aggregates all gated by the same caller-scoped filter (Lister Home: active campaigns / pending applications / pending submissions), expose ONE `SECURITY INVOKER` SQL function returning a typed row with all the counts rather than N PostgREST `.select('*', { count: 'exact', head: true })` calls. Three wins: (1) atomic — the numbers come from the same snapshot, no race where they disagree because they were fetched 20ms apart; (2) typed via `Database['public']['Functions']['<name>']['Returns'][number]` so the caller reads column names, not Range-header counts; (3) one network hop. Repeat the `<fkcol> = public.current_user_id()` filter inside EVERY sub-select even though RLS already applies — belt-and-suspenders so a future RLS regression doesn't silently inflate counts with other callers' rows. Pattern #42 still applies: `set search_path = ''` + schema-qualify enum casts (`'active'::public.listing_status`). Ref: `lister_dashboard_counts` in `us_048_lister_dashboard_counts_rpc.sql`.

- **`allowFontScaling={false}` vs `maxFontSizeMultiplier={1.3}` — they are NOT interchangeable.** Per docs/design.md §1.3 + §11 QA checklist: CONTENT text (body, captions, display-size numbers in stat tiles, card titles, anything informational) must honor system Dynamic Type up to 130% via `maxFontSizeMultiplier={1.3}`. UI CHROME (pills, chips, tab-bar labels, segment buttons — fixed-sized containers where scaling would break layout) opts out entirely with `allowFontScaling={false}`. The trap: a hero metric using `textStyles.display` LOOKS like chrome (big, standalone, inside a tile) but is CONTENT — the whole point of the screen. Getting this wrong makes the app inaccessible to users with large system text without any compile-time signal. Reviewer checklist: for every Text component, ask "does the meaning come from the value shown?" — if yes, `maxFontSizeMultiplier={1.3}`; if the text is a static UI label on a fixed-geometry element, `allowFontScaling={false}`. Ref: `StatTile` in `app/(lister)/dashboard.tsx`.

- **Zero-data state and empty-state are distinct UI states even when they share the same data shape.** A dashboard that renders `0 / 0 / 0` across three tiles when the user has nothing to show is a UX miss — design.md §5 specifies dedicated empty-state copy for every screen (Lister dashboard: "Post your first bounty.").
